# Spec Requirements Document

> Spec: Phase 2 — Intelligence Layer: Tagging & Sentiment
> Created: 2026-05-04
> Status: Planning

## Overview

Add a deterministic intelligence pipeline to AntarMind AI that automatically processes every submitted thought through text preprocessing, rule-based category tagging, and sentiment/intensity analysis — enriching each thought record with structured metadata without external AI dependencies.

## User Stories

### Automatically Categorized Thoughts

As a knowledge worker, I want each thought I log to be automatically tagged with one or more categories (e.g., Work, Stress, Trading), so that I can understand which domains dominate my thinking without manually labeling anything.

When a user submits "I'm stressed about my trading performance today", the system automatically assigns tags `["Stress", "Trading"]` and a sentiment of `Negative` with a high intensity score. These values appear on the thought card in the list, giving the user immediate structured feedback about their thought.

### Sentiment Awareness at a Glance

As a user, I want to see whether my thought carries a positive, negative, or neutral sentiment when I log it, so that I can build awareness of my emotional state throughout the day.

Each thought in the list displays a colored sentiment badge (green for Positive, red for Negative, grey for Neutral) alongside category tags. The user can scan their recent thoughts and immediately see the emotional tone of their day without reading every entry.

## Spec Scope

1. **Text Preprocessing Service** - Normalize raw thought text: lowercase, tokenize, remove punctuation, filter stop words, producing a clean token list for the tagging and sentiment engines.
2. **Rule-Based Tagging Engine** - Classify each thought into one or more of seven domains (Trading, Work, Health, Learning, Personal, Stress, Ideas) using configurable keyword dictionaries.
3. **Sentiment Analysis Service** - Detect Positive, Negative, or Neutral sentiment using a keyword scoring approach; produce an intensity score (0.0–1.0) based on emotional keyword weight.
4. **ThoughtEntry Enrichment** - Extend the `ThoughtEntry` model and storage schema to include `Tags`, `Sentiment`, and `IntensityScore`; run the pipeline automatically on `POST /api/thoughts`.
5. **GET /api/thoughts/{id} Endpoint** - New endpoint returning a single thought with its full enrichment metadata.
6. **Frontend Tag & Sentiment Display** - Update `ThoughtCard` to display category tag chips and a sentiment badge; update the thought list API call to include enriched fields.

## Out of Scope

- Machine learning or embedding-based classification (Phase 5)
- Pattern detection or trend analysis across multiple thoughts (Phase 3)
- User-defined custom categories or keyword customization
- Editing or re-tagging existing thoughts after submission
- Sentiment trend visualization charts (Phase 4)

## Expected Deliverable

1. A submitted thought is automatically tagged and sentiment-scored; the enriched data is returned in the `POST /api/thoughts` response and visible on the thought card in the UI.
2. The tagging engine correctly classifies test sentences across all seven domains; unit tests cover at least 3 positive/negative/ambiguous examples per domain.
3. Sentiment detection correctly identifies positive, negative, and neutral thoughts; intensity scores are non-zero for emotionally charged entries and near-zero for neutral entries.
4. `GET /api/thoughts/{id}` returns the thought with `tags`, `sentiment`, and `intensityScore` fields.
5. The preprocessing pipeline runs in under 50ms for any thought up to 2000 characters.
